title: Every PII-classified column must declare legal_basis and retention
statement: For every column in .devai/state/sensors/inventory_data_handling/data-model-pii.json#/tables[*].columns[*] whose `pii_class` field is set to a non-empty value, the column MUST also declare a non-empty `legal_basis` (LGPD Art. 7 / GDPR Art. 6 lawful basis) and a non-empty `retention` policy. Heuristic auto-classification of PII (set by sense-data-handling) does NOT itself satisfy the invariant — the legal_basis and retention values must be authored by a human with appropriate authority (Owner for legal_basis; Architect for retention).
rationale: Stynx-side specialization of DEVAI's INV-INVENTORY-002. Phase A of C-4 surfaced one currently-violating column — record.email, heuristically classified as `contact` PII — missing both legal_basis and retention. The 'gate' severity reflects that LGPD/GDPR compliance review cannot complete with these fields blank: a lawyer reviewing the data inventory needs to see the lawful basis declared at the column level, not inferred. The fix is either (a) author legal_basis + retention for record.email, OR (b) demonstrate via an `inv-override` annotation that record.email is in fact not PII (with rationale). Promoted from INV-CANDIDATE-01KRQD0PQV47VSE79RAW1FTEVQ.
